hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic complementarity at position 5 and introducing hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will improve SSTR2 selectivity by strengthening interactions with SSTR2-unique ECL2/TM5 residues while reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that structural instability is the primary barrier to generating viable candidates. Tightening structural constraints and focusing mutations on non-pharmacophore positions should improve both structural quality and selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Structural failures suggest excessive clashes in the binding pocket. Tightening the clash threshold will enforce better packing and reduce structural instability.
To improve selectivity, we must disrupt off-target contacts at non-pharmacophore positions (1/2/5/6/11/12) while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT at 7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.
